content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN, and a string SSS of length KKK consisting of characters L\texttt{L}L and R\texttt{R}R only.

You have to perform KKK operations on the array. In the ithi^{th}ith operation:


If the array is of size 111, you do not need to perform the operation.
Else, choose an integer XXX (1≤X<∣A∣)(1\le X \lt |A|)(1≤X<∣A∣) randomly and equi-probably and break the array AAA into subarrays A[1,X]A[1, X]A[1,X] and A[X+1,∣A∣]A[X+1, |A|]A[X+1,∣A∣], where ∣A∣|A|∣A∣ denotes the length of the array.

If Si=LS_i=\texttt{L}Si​=L, set A:=A[1,X]A:=A[1, X]A:=A[1,X]
If Si=RS_i=\texttt{R}Si​=R, set A:=A[X+1,∣A∣]A:=A[X+1, |A|]A:=A[X+1,∣A∣]


Find the expected sum of all elements in the final array AAA after KKK operations.

The value can be represented as an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).

Note that A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the size of the array and the number of operations respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the elements of the array.
The third line of each test case contains a string of length KKK consisting only of characters L\texttt{L}L and R\texttt{R}R.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the expected sum of all elements in the final array AAA after KKK operations.


The value can be represented as an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500500500.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 500500500.
**Subtask 1 (30 points):** The sum of NNN and the sum of KKK across all test cases won't exceed 100100100.
